mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-unemployment periods exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of cases or t-test p-value > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in CPI inflation and stagnation in federal funds rate signals central bank inaction, creating negative SPX returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in this regime exceed 5% in 60%+ of cases or interaction term coefficient < 0.1 in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% quarterly decline in GDP growth signals weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low-GDP scenarios exceed 3% in 50%+ of cases or R-squared < 0.15 in demand-return regression.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k monthly signals robust labor market demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-payroll scenarios fall below 1% in 40%+ of cases or t-test p-value > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A 1.5% monthly drop in industrial production signals supply-side contraction, leading to negative SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low-industrial scenarios exceed 2% in 30%+ of cases or Rank IC < 0.05 over 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales signals strong consumer demand, producing positive SPX returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high-demand scenarios fall below 4% in 50%+ of cases or interaction term coefficient < 0.2 in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% monthly rise in CPI alongside stable Fed rates signals inflation persistence, creating negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in this regime exceed 3% in 40%+ of cases or interaction term coefficient < 0.15 in regression.
